title: Partner/adopter + safety-reviewer outreach; candidate protocol shortlist
context: The project's headline outcome metric is real external adoption (metric #1: ≥5 distinct adopters), and all tasks currently carry verifiedNeed:false because no lab, core facility, or repository has committed to using the output. Simultaneously, the safety-reviewer role (task reviewer-002) requires recruiting from biosafety/EHS professional networks. This outreach task identifies candidate partners and safety reviewers and produces a shortlist of candidate protocols (low-to-moderate hazard, openly licensed) to pitch to those partners.
objective: Conduct structured outreach to candidate adopting partners (labs, core facilities, protocol repositories) and biosafety/EHS professionals, document candidate responses, and compile a shortlist of low-to-moderate-hazard protocols to prioritize for authoring based on partner interest.
acceptanceCriteria: At least five candidate adopting organizations or individuals (labs, core facilities, protocols.io collection maintainers, teaching programs, open-science nonprofits) contacted and responses recorded.
At least three candidate credentialed safety reviewers contacted; their credentials, affiliation, and availability assessed and recorded.
A candidate protocol shortlist of ≥10 low-to-moderate-hazard cancer-research protocols is compiled, with per-protocol source, license (verified or pending), and partner interest level.
Outreach document committed to the project repo; commit DCO signed-off.
No verifiedNeed is claimed until a named partner commits in writing (M1 gate).
resources: Reproducibility Project / Center for Open Science — https://www.cos.io/
output: A committed outreach document recording candidate partners, candidate safety reviewers, and a ranked candidate-protocol shortlist with license and interest-level notes; commit DCO signed-off.
